reasoning:
As of mid-April 2026, the combined market capitalization of USDT and USDC is roughly $262.7 billion (USDT at ~$184.3 billion and USDC at ~$78.4 billion). In order to reach the $300 billion threshold by June 30, 2026, the combined market caps would need to grow by roughly $37.3 billion in just 2.5 months. This would require an average growth rate of about $15 billion per month. 

Recent historic data shows that over the past six months (between October 2025, when the total was ~$250 billion, and April 2026), the combined market cap only grew by approximately $12-13 billion in total, which equates to an average growth of ~$2 billion per month. Absent an unprecedented catalyst or extreme parabolic blow-off top causing massive instantaneous liquidity inflows, achieving a growth rate of 7x the recent monthly average over the coming weeks is highly unlikely. Considering these factors, I assign a very low probability of 6% to the combined market cap surpassing $300 billion by June 30, 2026.
